title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
description:
## Merge Two Sorted Lists

You are given two sorted linked lists (as plain integer lists). Merge them into one sorted linked list and return the result as a plain integer list.

**Constraints:**
- The number of nodes in each list is in the range `[0, 50]`.
- `-100 <= Node.val <= 100`
- Both `list1` and `list2` are sorted in non-decreasing order.

solutions.label: Iterative with Dummy Head — O(m+n)
solutions.explanation: Use a dummy head to simplify the logic of building the merged list. At each step, compare the front nodes of both lists and append the smaller one. After one list is exhausted, attach the remaining nodes of the other (they're already sorted). The dummy node avoids special-casing the head.

solutions.label: Recursive — O(m+n)
solutions.explanation: Recursive merge: if either list is empty, return the other. Otherwise, the smaller head becomes the result; its next is the merge of the remaining lists. Elegant but uses O(m+n) call stack space vs O(1) for the iterative approach.

solutions.label: Iterative with Dummy Head — O(m+n)
solutions.explanation: Use a dummy head to simplify building the merged list. At each step compare the front nodes and append the smaller one. After one list is exhausted, attach the remaining nodes.

hints: Use a dummy head node to simplify building the result — avoids special-casing the first node.
At each step, take the smaller of the two front nodes. When one list runs out, append the rest of the other.
